# Verify Product Hunt Launch Theatre

## Claim being tested

A product launch won because a community genuinely liked it.

## Alternative hypothesis

The launch rank was inflated by coordinated voting, mass outreach, or reciprocal communities.

## Data fields

- voter/commenter ID if public
- vote/comment timestamps
- comment text
- maker network
- external referral waves
- social posts asking for support
- disclosure
- retention / usage after launch

## Metrics

- vote burst ratio
- comment depth and specificity
- voter account age/activity
- maker-network overlap
- vote-to-comment ratio
- post-launch retention vs launch rank
- reciprocal support graph

## Graph model

Voter-product bipartite graph over multiple launches. Identify communities of accounts that repeatedly support the same maker circles.

## Safe conclusion

"The launch shows rank-manufacturing risk where engagement is bursty, low-context, and clustered around social pressure or reciprocal support."
